bullets: Designed a Community Health Score with 4 dimensions (engagement, quality, responsiveness, risk) tracking 60+ open-source projects across Reddit, GitHub, and Stack Exchange.
Used sentiment analysis and topic modeling on roughly 80K posts and issues to surface what was actually moving each community up or down.
Shipped a Power BI dashboard that flagged 12 at-risk communities for the program team to look into first, saving them several days of manual triage per quarter.
Owned 6 Power BI dashboards covering sales, operations, and customer KPIs that 20+ stakeholders relied on weekly.
Automated recurring performance reports and cut manual reporting time by about 30%, freeing the team for actual analysis.
Worked with marketing and supply-chain leads on stock planning, cost control, and loyalty campaigns. The cohort retention work became the basis for the loyalty program rollout.
Reorganized KPI definitions across teams so the same metric meant the same thing in every room. Cut the volume of 'why does this number not match' threads in half.
Tuned Amazon ad spend across 40+ SKUs and brought ACOS down by about 20% over 3 months.
Ran competitor and category research that supported the launch of 10 new self-care products.
Found and sized 3 product gaps in the category that the team built into the next quarter's roadmap.
